design_philosophy.core_loop: Pocket balls in sequence or into flashing star pockets to earn points and advance through levels; manage limited lives by avoiding scratches and consecutive misses
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (lives) balanced against difficulty escalation (increasing ball counts per level); precision execution under pressure
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Authentic billiards/pool simulation with skill expression through shot angles, spin application (english), and trick shot execution
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Treats pool as a legitimate sport simulation rather than arcade abstraction; penalizes missed shots and scratches harshly; requires genuine understanding of billiards mechanics (english, massé, jump shots) rather than button-mashing

difficulty.lives_system: NES version starts with 5 lives; one life lost per scratch or two consecutive failed shots in single-player Pocket Game mode
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression; each of 4 levels has predetermined score threshold to advance; flashing star bonuses can award extra lives mid-run
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Two-player mode removes lives system entirely (failures simply pass control); trick shot challenges offer optional difficulty scaling with variable complexity

progression.unlock_mechanism: Score thresholds gate advancement through 4 sequential levels; each level increases ball count on the rack, escalating complexity
replayability_hooks: Multiple game modes (Pocket Game, 9-Ball Game, Trick Shots)
Two-player competitive play with different rule sets
Skill-based execution rewards multiple playstyles (conservative vs. aggressive shot selection)
Flashing star pocket bonuses create variable reward moments

world_building_techniques: Character design (homage to 'The Color of Money' film in Genesis/SNES versions)
Location theming (Las Vegas, New York City, Atlantic City in enhanced remakes)

danger_signals: Harsh life penalty system encourages tense, focused play sessions
Skill-execution based difficulty may frustrate less-coordinated players
environmental_storytelling: Billiards hall aesthetic with photo-realistic backgrounds in enhanced versions
Pool table configuration variations (six-ball, nine-ball, different racks) signal different challenge contexts
